Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Simple Flying Podcast Episode 284: Air Canada Crash Latest, United Airlines Introduces Couches In Economy Class

Join Tom & Channing for the latest aviation hot topics.

Air Canada's recent incident and United's economy cabin reconfiguration signal industry-wide pressures to address both safety concerns and seating density economics simultaneously.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion and public unveiling mark the final validation phase before launch, signaling NASA's readiness to deploy a transformative infrared observatory.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

NASA's Artemis II countdown marks a critical validation of SLS and Orion systems before human lunar landing attempts, with international partnership through Canadian astronaut participation signaling expanded deep-space exploration accessibility.

See NASA →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Booking Last-Minute Business Class Award Tickets: Everything You Need To Know

Last-minute business class award tickets can offer significant savings relative to purchasing the fares in cash, although this is not guaranteed.

Award availability for premium cabins often peaks within 48-72 hours of departure as airlines release unsold inventory, creating arbitrage opportunities for loyalty program members over cash purchasers.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety paradigm from investigating accidents to systematically preventing them through continuous risk identification and mitigation.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding as it looks to expand its…...

AI-powered pilot training platforms are attracting significant venture capital as carriers seek scalable alternatives to traditional flight instruction.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Cold chain logistics for ISS research requires specialized coordination between ground teams and payload developers to maintain experiment viability during multi-day cargo missions.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Airbus Tests Multi-Aircraft Aerial Firefighting System

Trial linked aircraft, drones and ground crews for enhanced targeting and coordination.

Networked aerial systems could dramatically improve wildfire suppression efficiency by enabling real-time coordination between manned aircraft, drones, and ground teams.

See AVweb →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

The SMS Declaration of Compliance creates a formal regulatory checkpoint requiring operators to document their safety management systems meet FAA standards, not just implement them.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Russian YouTuber killed after airplane may have been mistaken for Ukraine drone

Russian aviation blogger Pavel Koshkin was killed when the Alto NG aircraft he was flying crashed near Kolomna,…...

The incident highlights how heightened air-defense alertness during conflict can create fatal risks for civilian aviation operators in disputed airspace.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 26, 2026

Departing Geneva toward the Matterhorn.

Alpine terrain operations require specialized departure procedures that leverage geographic features for instrument approach planning and crew situational awareness.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

“Please Do Something”: Pilots Have Complained About LaGuardia Airport’s Safety For Years

Pilots at LaGuardia Airport have reported multiple near-miss situations due to unclear instructions from air traffic control.

Systemic ATC instruction clarity failures at major hub airports create liability exposure and operational risk that regulatory intervention has failed to resolve proactively.

See Simple Flying →

▶
General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...

Frederick Municipal Airport's diverse traffic profile from trainers to corporate jets demonstrates how secondary airports serve critical roles in regional aviation ecosystems beyond major hubs.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

House Committee Advances ALERT Act

Measure includes collision-mitigation requirements, changes for military aircraft operations and limits on some ADS-B data use.

The legislation restricts ADS-B data accessibility while mandating collision-avoidance technology, affecting both civilian operators and military flight operations simultaneously.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

This Airline Has The World’s Most Comfortable Premium Economy Seat In 2026

The Dubai-based carrier's premium economy product is relatively new.

Emirates' newly refined premium economy demonstrates how established carriers are investing in mid-tier cabin differentiation to capture higher margins between economy and business class.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find

For the second consecutive year, winter sea ice in the Arctic reached a level that matches the lowest peak observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979. On March 15, Arctic sea ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles (14.29 million square kilometers), very close to the 2025 peak of 5.53...

Arctic sea ice decline directly impacts aircraft routing, de-icing fluid requirements, and operational costs for polar aviation corridors increasingly used for transatlantic flights.

See NASA →

▶
General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Medium-hub airports like CID serve critical regional roles by accommodating diverse operations from flight training to commercial carriers, supporting both pilot development and air service connectivity.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 26, 2026

5 Structural Advantages Of The A-10 Warthog For Close Air Support Missions

This aircraft offers some impressive offensive and defensive capabilities.

The A-10's redundant systems and armor distribution enable sustained operations in high-threat environments where other aircraft would be mission-limited or lost.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Authorities Searching For American Airlines Flight Attendant Reported Missing In Colombia

The disappearance of an American Airlines flight attendant in Medellin, Colombia, has left his family desperate for answers.

The incident underscores security risks airline crew members face during layovers in high-crime international destinations, prompting potential policy reviews on crew safety protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy...

IXPE's X-ray polarimetry data on RCW 86 provides unique angular momentum insights unavailable from traditional telescopes, advancing astrophysical understanding.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers near-term environmental gains without waiting for next-generation aircraft technology maturation.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Colorado’s Rocky Mountain Metro Airport Launches Unleaded Avgas

UL94 sales begin with state-backed price support and STC reimbursement.

State-backed pricing and STC reimbursement incentives demonstrate a regulatory model to accelerate unleaded fuel adoption beyond federal mandate requirements.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Only 54% Full: Delta Air Lines' 10 Emptiest Long-Haul Routes Revealed

How many of them can you guess? There are some intriguing surprises.

Delta's underutilized long-haul routes suggest capacity misallocation opportunities and potential network restructuring to improve load factors and profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic fighter assembly capability signals strategic shift toward aerospace self-sufficiency and reduces dependency on foreign military aviation suppliers.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Dreamliner groundings push Kenya Airways back into the red

Kenya Airways returned to financial losses in 2025, the airline reported on March 24, 2026. The Kenyan flag…...

Aircraft availability disruptions can rapidly reverse airline profitability gains, highlighting supply chain vulnerability for African carriers with limited fleet diversity.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

AMA Advocacy: H.R. 6460 passes U.S. House of Representatives

On Tuesday, March 24, H.R. 6460, a bill championed by AMA that will simplify altitude...

Passage of H.R. 6460 removes regulatory barriers for drone operators by streamlining altitude reporting requirements, potentially accelerating commercial UAS integration into controlled airspace.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain

Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Friday, March 20, 2026 Curiosity has just concluded a very intense week of science observations and engineering activities, as it wraps up its monthslong investigation...

Curiosity's extended boxwork terrain investigation demonstrates sustained rover productivity in Mars exploration, validating long-term autonomous mission planning for future planetary rovers.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

FAA document requests signal potential enforcement action beyond typical oversight, suggesting California's funding practices may violate federal airport grant conditions or statutory requirements.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's Artemis II lunar mission targets April 1 launch with extensive live coverage, marking humanity's return to Moon exploration after 50+ years.

See NASA →

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

360-degree FPV drones with enterprise-grade sensors enable safer autonomous flight in complex environments, expanding commercial applications beyond traditional piloting.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's 3,500 monthly departures from Austin signals a major hub expansion that will significantly increase crew scheduling complexity and gate utilization across the airport.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

Pentagon's potential reallocation of Ukraine-destined Patriot interceptors to Middle East operations reveals competing geopolitical defense priorities that could impact aircraft protection capabilities across multiple conflict zones.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Consistent cabin crew excellence has become Virgin Australia's competitive differentiator in a crowded regional market, suggesting service quality directly impacts long-term brand valuation.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

ATL's 100M+ annual passengers underscore the critical infrastructure demands on major hubs as connecting points for domestic and international networks.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Comet spin-reversal mechanisms revealed by Hubble demonstrate dynamic forces affecting small celestial bodies, with implications for space mission trajectory planning and asteroid deflection strategies.

See NASA →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian forces have adapted vintage Yak-52 trainers as improvised air-defense platforms, demonstrating how legacy aircraft can be repurposed for modern asymmetric warfare when advanced air defenses are unavailable.

See The Aviationist →
